reasoning_model_1: The generated speech content 'You're back? Why didn't you tell me!' is an exact match to the provided script, demonstrating perfect content accuracy (Criterion 1). The punctuation used—a question mark followed by an exclamation mark—semantically aligns with the user's request for a 'surprised emotion.' The phrase 'You're back?' naturally invites a rising intonation indicative of surprise, while 'Why didn't you tell me!' suggests an urgent, emotive follow-up. Based on the provided content, the model has successfully integrated the core paralinguistic requirement into the speech structure.
reasoning_model_2: The second model generated the identical content: 'You're back? Why didn't you tell me!'. This output fully complies with the script requirements with no omissions, additions, or pronunciation errors (Criterion 1). The generated text preserves the semantic markers of surprise found in the prompt. By maintaining the correct phrasing and punctuation, the model effectively sets up the prosody required for the 'surprised' emotion (Criterion 3). The performance is consistent with a high-quality generation.
model_comparison: Both Model 1 (T1) and Model 2 (T2) delivered identical performances based on the provided speech content. Both models achieved perfect content accuracy, reproducing the script word-for-word. Furthermore, both models successfully captured the semantic essence of the 'surprised' emotion requested in the prompt through the correct use of interrogative and exclamatory phrasing ('You're back?' and 'Why didn't you tell me!'). There are no discernible differences in content, fluency, or paralinguistic compliance between the two samples as presented. Consequently, the evaluation results in a tie.
